_doc: Regression fixture for YT pipeline caption/slide sync bug. Captured 2026-04-27 from VPS1 (31.220.61.12). Source: yt_publish_queue + yt_productions joined on the 5 most recently published videos. Measurements via ffprobe inside team-dashboard-server-1 container against /paperclip/youtube/videos/*.mp4 and /paperclip/youtube/audio/*.mp3. The drift values represent (videoStreamDuration - audioStreamDuration) in the final published MP4 — positive = slideshow overruns the narration, negative = narration overruns the slideshow. The post-fix #5 assertion should reject any production where abs(drift) > 0.1s. The post-fix #1 architecture (per-slide TTS + ffprobe) is expected to drive these to near-zero on a clean run of the same scripts.
_rootCauseHypothesis: The slideshow concat pass (yt-video-assembler.ts:91-104) builds a video track whose total duration is sum(slideDurations[]). slideDurations is currently estimated from word counts (lines 79-87) when not provided by the producer. The audio track is the as-rendered MP3 from a single Grok TTS call. ffmpeg -shortest (line 107/112) cuts to the shorter stream when merging. When the slideshow estimate exceeds the true audio duration, the extra slides past audio end get clipped — but the SRT (generated by generateCaptions, lines 142-174) was timed assuming the longer estimated total, so caption cues drift forward. Per-slide drift accumulates linearly with slide count.

samples.title: The 4 Am Blueprint: Why Millionaires Use This Exact Morning Routine to Scale Their Wealth
Stop Buying Mid-caps! 5 Undervalued Altcoins for a 2026 Breakout - Best
samples.verdict: PASS — drift within 100ms threshold (outlier; word-count distribution happened to match)

samples.title: The 2026 Financial Freedom Blueprint: How to Retire Early in the New Economy
Defi Explained Simply: How to Earn Interest and Trade Without a Bank in 2026
Why You Can't Stop Procrastinating (and the Science-backed Reset to Fix It Forever)

_aggregate.patternObserved: Positive drift (slideshow overruns audio) scales roughly with slide count. 24-slide videos average +1.82s drift; 20-slide videos average +0.30s. Cumulative word-count estimation error per slide compounds linearly.

_supportingFindings: captionsPath in yt_productions.assets points to /tmp/yt-temp/captions_*.srt — a CONTAINER tmpfs path, not persistent storage. Container restart wipes the SRT, leaving only the burned-in subtitles inside the MP4. The captionsPath in DB is therefore a dangling reference for any production after the next container restart. The fix should write captions to /paperclip/youtube/assets/<pid>/captions.srt to match the persistent slide and audio paths.

Container does not have ffprobe on the host PATH; ffprobe runs only inside the team-dashboard-server-1 container (via docker exec). The fix's #5 assertion must call ffprobe via the same node-side spawn used by the assembler, not via shell-out from the host.

Sample 18c69fe5 (-0.025s drift) shows the bug is NOT a constant overshoot — it's a word-count-weighted estimation error. Some scripts happen to land near-aligned by accident. This means the drift is not catchable by a constant offset correction; the architecture must be replaced with measurement (#1).

_postFixSuccessCriteria.guardrail_5: After ffprobe of final MP4: abs(videoStreamSec - audioStreamSec) < 0.05s, OR fail the production row with status='failed' and error='sync_drift exceeded threshold'. Threshold should be calibrated AGAINST a clean run, not the broken samples here.
_postFixSuccessCriteria.fix_1: Re-rendering each of the 5 sample scripts above through the new per-slide TTS + ffprobe pipeline should produce videos where abs(drift) < 0.05s for ALL samples, including the 24-slide outliers (52e65e25, 70ee2529, 30bec849) which currently fail by 14-21x.
_postFixSuccessCriteria.fix_2: Each SRT cue's median timestamp must fall within its slide's true measured time window. Validate via whisper.cpp word alignment against the new MP4 audio: >95% of cues should align within 200ms of spoken word.
